    Home › Manufacturers › Airbus

    Airbus

    Europe · Founded 1970

    The European consortium that took half the world's airliner market.

    Aircraft here8
    Longest75.36 m
    Combined built15,920
    First flights1972–2018
    The company

    The story so far

    Airbus began in 1970 as a political improbability: a consortium of French, German, British and Spanish partners pooling their aircraft industries to challenge American dominance. Its first product, the A300, was the world's first twin-engine widebody — an aircraft the established manufacturers said the market did not want.

    Two decades later Airbus had pioneered fly-by-wire in airliners with the A320, and by the 2000s it was building the largest passenger aircraft ever flown. Today it splits the world's airliner market with Boeing almost evenly, and its aircraft on this site range from the A300 that started it all to the A380 and the composite A350.

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
